programmes.show_description: How do they make the king of cheeses, Parmesan? How do they keep the runways running at the world's busiest airports? How do they manufacture relaxing reclining chairs?
How do they weave a Turkish carpet? How do they use helicopter blade technology for cross country skis, and control the water at Niagara Falls?
Triumph Stag. Mike tries to find a rust-free classic Stag, but it's harder than he thinks. Ed will have to keep costs super low for any chance of a profit on this one.
Lotus Elan. The boys are challenged to find a Lotus Elan that hasn't been snapped up by restorers. Once they've tracked one down, Mike falls in love after minutes behind the wheel.
Riding Shotgun. Henry and Sam are in Suffolk, checking out a collection of vintage bikes. Henry has his eye on petrol pumps, but Sam has other ideas after seeing the sidecars.
Spoilt For Choice. In Lowestoft, Henry and Sam can't believe their luck when they check out Charlie's wares. He has everything from cars and bikes to stationary engines.
Follow the production process of fresh cut flowers, adhesive tape and tofu, and find out how each of these items is made.
Follow the production process of filing cabinets and blown glass, and find out how each of these items is made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Invitation Only. Wayne Carini welcomes viewers into high-end car collecting. He presents an exclusive look at the hottest parties occurring at the Pebble Beach Concours d'Elegance.
Cannonball's Run. Wayne is hired to sell automotive journalist Brock Yates' Eliminator roadster; the vehicle is picked up from the Saratoga Automobile Museum and taken to auction.
Back to the Beach. Derek Hill, the son of legendary auto racer Phil Hill, brings his father's 1912 Packard to the Pebble Beach Concours d'Elegance.
Backyard Finds. Wayne hunts for more high-end beauties by exploring old stomping grounds, where he uncovers numerous Mopar cars, a 1905 Cadillac and a Ferrari 330.
Alfa Romeo Alfasud. Mike takes a risk by buying a late Alfasud 1.5 TI at auction. With no service history, dodgy brakes, and clapped out suspension, can Edd work his magic?
1963 Volvo PV544. Mike was challenged to find a classic endurance sports car and the Volvo PV544 fit the bill. Mike finds a running example, but with an engine that few can master.
Saab 9-3. Mike wants a high performance car with an historic badge, but it's hard to find a Saab 9-3 Turbo at a good price. With tight margins will Edd have any room for manoeuvre?
Renault Alpine. Mike is in France to hunt down the rare Renault Alpine 310. The car he finds is great, but it has some surprises in store, and Edd is charged with getting it fixed.
Karmann Ghia. Mike is in Idaho to buy the ultimate retro sportster, the Karmann Ghia. Edd loves it, but will the broken gearbox and shoddy interior stop them making a profit?
2000 Porsche 996. Mike is excited to pick up one of his favourite types of convertible: the 2000 Porsche 996. With only a few issues, this car will be an absolute steal for him.
2004 BMW M3. Mike finds a BMW six-cylinder modern classic, the E46 M3, with the subframe already fixed. He challenges Ant to transform the transmission with new tech.
Triumph Spitfire. In Gloucestershire, Henry and Fuzz take on a risky Triumph Spitfire that has been in a crash. In their biggest restoration challenge yet, can they fix it?
Henry tackles his biggest project, restoring a Bell 47 helicopter for the 80th Anniversary Flight. It's a race against time to get it off the ground.
Old Mates And Motor-Racing. Richard gets into hot water as he tries to upgrade his racing licence and the Cogs head to the Isle of Man TT to help launch a new British motorbike.
MotoGP: Brazil Sprint Race. All the action from the second Sprint Race of the 2026 MotoGP World Championship, at the Autódromo Internacional de Goiânia in Brazil.
Henry Cole and the team take on a classic 1950s Massey Ferguson combine harvester in one of their biggest restorations yet.
The expert restorers breathe new life into beloved family heirlooms: a mug with a pottery frog inside, a model windmill and a marching bass drum.
Jugtastic! Angus visits a home with a pub in it, filled with over 160 jugs. Later, he encounters a colossal collection of Glen Millar Vinyl and V-discs!
Last Chance Dig. The Blacklighters have just one week left to strike opal before the summer heat shuts them down. The Bushmen scramble to beat mechanical failures.
The Gibsons. Paul Mackie and his team of experts travel to the unforgiving Western Australian Pilbara to help an Indigenous family gold mine save itself from financial disaster.
Mystery On Easter Island. Archaeologists use new digital technology to understand the ancient moai statues of Easter Island and the purpose behind their mysterious placement.
Maze Of Cryptic Terror. Archaeologists use state-of-the-art digital technology to uncover the truth behind a cave wall in Nottinghamshire, England. What do the unusual symbols represent?
Discover how everyday items such as turntables, steam engines, playground equipment and non-stick pans are made.
How do they make garden forks, English toffee, paint chip cards, and Bundt pans? Find out as more everyday items are put under the microscope.
More everyday items are put under the microscope as we discover how they're made.
High-precision cutting tools and stained glass are put under the microscope to discover how they were made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry and Simon head to the Isle of Wight to meet some former publicans who have a wide variety of hoarded items. Can the boys find some lost treasures?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: On the Isle of Wight, Henry and Simon meet another Simon, who has an extensive collection of old vehicles, including a retro three-wheeled motorbike and an American rocker.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry and Simon head to the most Easterly point of Britain, Ness Point in Lowestoft. There they find an absolutely packed shed full of assorted chairs and other furniture.
Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth begin their hunt for antiques in Melrose. Who will make the most profit at the first auction in the Lake District?
Angus Ashworth and Steven Moore embark on the second leg of their trip in Alva. Steven uncovers a carved horse, while Angus buys a beautiful coffee grinder.
Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth are on the lookout for valuable antiques in Crossford. Plus, Angus visits a ground-breaking swimming club in Glasgow.
Steven Moore and Angus Ashworth scour the town of Belper for interesting antiques. Plus, Angus visits one of the oldest underwear factories in the world.
Antiques experts Angus Ashworth and Steven Moore embark on the last leg of their road trip. Who will be victorious at the final auction in Dundee?
Wood restoration specialist Will brings a precious but broken toy yacht back to life. Plus, leather expert Suzie restores a midwifery bag used in the First World War.
Wood restorer specialist Will restores a handmade childhood chair. Plus, bookbinder Chris revives a treasured wedding album that was damaged in a house fire.
Soft toy restorers Julie and Amanda revive a threadbare toy rabbit. Drum expert Pete restores a battered bass drum and furniture restorer Will works on a wooden church pew.
In Scotland, Angus and the team clear out an attic filled with Ferrari memorabilia. How much will this incredible collection make at auction?
In the seaside town of Filey, Angus and the team help a couple clear out their home. Angus sees potential in some vintage toys and boxes of old soap.
Angus helps the ex-EastEnders star Samantha Womack shift her showbiz things, and former MP Alan Johnson parts with a chess set and much-loved guitar.
Leather expert Suzie and furniture restorer Will join forces to revive a strap chair. Mechanical maestro Steve faces a tricky fix on a motorised toy warplane.
The experts give a precious clock its chime again, repair a ring which contains a beloved mother's ashes, and make an old chair swivel again.
Wood restoration specialist Will brings a precious but broken toy yacht back to life. Plus, leather expert Suzie restores a midwifery bag used in the First World War.
Wood restorer specialist Will restores a handmade childhood chair. Plus, bookbinder Chris revives a treasured wedding album that was damaged in a house fire.
